plant.description: Unique History: Classified under the 'Ashmari-hara' (stone-breaking) and 'Mutra-virechaniya' (diuretic) groups in ancient treatises like Charaka Samhita. Historically used to symbolise the 'shattering of blocks'. Found throughout India and Southeast Asia and cultivated for medicine. Physical Characteristics: A perennial, small herb growing in the sub-alpine and alpine regions of the Himalayas (altitude 2500m to 4000m). It features broad, heart-shaped (cordate) lower leaves and narrow, amplexicaul upper leaves. Found in rock crevices and sandy soils throughout India and tropical South Asia. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Bergenin, D-mannitol, and Benzoic acid. The presence of bergenin is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-inflammatory action on the heart. Strict Standardizations: The rhizome is standardized in API based on its greyish-brown color and characteristic extremely bitter-pungent-biting-astringent taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<10%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%). HPLC monitors bergenin levels.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful pacifier of Vata and Kapha doshas. Due to its Kashaya (astringent) and Tikta (bitter) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Ashmari-hara' (stone-breaking) and 'Mutra-virechaniya' (diuretic) therapies. It clears the localized Kapha while balancing the dry Vata.

rhizome_powder.method: 3-6g of fine powder taken with milk or honey (for kidney health) or warm water.

root_decoction.method: 20g of dried root boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic asthma and bronchial congestion. Take twice daily before meals.

cold_infusion.method: 10g of crushed rhizome soaked in 60ml water overnight; filtered and taken for managing thirst and acidity.

pashanabheda_asava.method: 15-30ml of fermented extract taken twice daily after meals for managing chronic gut infections and general weakness.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey once or twice daily for respiratory support and cooling during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Arishta: 15-30 ml, divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm milk at bedtime.

medicinal_uses: World-Class Stone and Kidney Specialist (Ashmari-hara): Pashanabheda is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for urinary and kidney stones. Its name literally translates to 'stone-breaker'. Its 'Bhedana' (breaching) and 'Tikshna' (sharp) nature provided by bergenin helps in breaking down the stones and providing systemic stabilization of the urinary tract. WHO-aligned research confirms its potent anti-urolithiatic and diuretic effects.

Urinary and Kidney Care: As a mild diuretic, it helps in relieving urinary tract infections, burning urination (Mutra-kricchra), and localized edema (Shotha) by clearing Kapha-related obstructions in the bladder and kidneys.

Anti-inflammatory and Pain Relief: Systemically used for conditions like chronic inflammatory conditions and joint pain by clearing blocked Vata pathways and reducing local swelling through its cooling property. It effectively manages 'Ashmari-shula' (renal colic).

Skin Health and Wound Healing: Its antimicrobial and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic skin conditions and localized inflammation. The rhizome paste applied topically promotes rapid relief from acne and sun-burns while providing systemic 'Vranaropana' (wound-healing) effects.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Iridoid Glycosides (Bergenin, Nyctanthin), Flavonoids (Astragalin, Nicotiflorin), and Tannins. Bergenin is the primary bioactive responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory and diuretic action on the heart and kidneys.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and aquatic plant with a history of use spanning millennia across various global cultures and traditional systems without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Kapha should use it in moderation as it is Sheeta (cooling). Avoid during pregnancy in very large therapeutic doses except under professional guidance.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-hypertensive or anticoagulant medications as it may potentiate their effects.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
